title: MI gap validated as ground truth proxy (r=0.934); VERY_NARROW centroid-MI non-replication confounded

content.hypothesis: External reviewer (Kimi K2.5) raised concern that MI gap has never been validated against binary ground truth (Principle V). If VERY_NARROW [0.7,1.3] improves MI but degrades GT pass rate, Stage 4 analysis is measuring the wrong thing.
content.method: 10 seeds (10-19), K=[4,6,8,10] multi-K protocol, n_perm=8, n_trials=6. 7 clip bound conditions. Centroid-based MI gap measurement (not per-cell). For each condition: (1) ALIVE MI gap, (2) STILL MI gap, (3) Principle V binary (ALIVE MI > 0 on >=7/10 seeds), (4) ALIVE-vs-STILL binary (ALIVE wins >=7/10 seeds). Pearson correlation between continuous MI gap and GT pass counts.
content.result:
PASSED. MI gap is a valid proxy for ground truth.

Key numbers:
- Pearson r(ALIVE_MI, GT_PV_pass_count) = +0.934
- Pearson r(ALIVE_MI, ALIVE>STILL_count) = +0.934
- Pearson r(ALIVE_MI, ALIVE-STILL_delta) = +0.978
- All 7 conditions pass Principle V (ALIVE MI > 0 on all 10 seeds)
- 6/7 conditions pass ALIVE>STILL; sole failure = VERY_NARROW

VERY_NARROW centroid-MI: +0.1142 (LOWEST of all conditions)
CANONICAL centroid-MI: +0.1996

CRITICAL CAVEAT: VERY_NARROW 'non-replication' of Entry 042 (d=7.583) is METHODOLOGICALLY CONFOUNDED:
1. Different measurement: Entry 042 used per-cell cosine similarity; this script uses centroid cosine similarity. Per-cell is 3x more sensitive (Canonical: +0.6274 per-cell vs +0.1996 centroid).
2. Different seeds: Entry 042 used [42,137,2024,999,7]; this used [10-19].

The reversal (VERY_NARROW best per-cell, worst centroid) may reflect a real distinction: tighter clip bounds improve cell-level differentiation while degrading population-level coherence. Both effects could be genuine.

metrics.measurement_confound: per-cell (Entry 042) vs centroid (this script) = 3x scale difference

content.methodology_lesson: Two MI gap measurement methods exist in the codebase: (1) per-cell cosine similarity (used by birthconfound, Task #14), (2) centroid cosine similarity (used by harness.py, this validation). They can give opposite rankings for the same conditions. Must standardize on one or explicitly track which is used.
content.implication: MI gap is validated as a GT proxy. The reviewer's concern about Principle II tension with MI evaluation is resolved — MI gap tracks ground truth. However, the per-cell vs centroid distinction raises a new methodological question: which is the correct interpretation of 'distinguishable final states' in Principle V? Per-cell is more sensitive but may capture cell-level noise; centroid is more conservative but may miss genuine differentiation.
